Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately. ***

Reach for the discount, and the customer pays $170 for the table and $127.50 for the chairs. The total amount is $297.50. The promotional week not only helps the factory workers but also encourages customers to support local businesses. The customer saves 15% on each item, which makes the shopping experience more rewarding. The discount is applied to each item separately, so the savings are calculated individually for the table and the chairs. The customer pays $170 for the table and $127.50 for the chairs. The total amount is $297.50. The promotional week not only helps the factory workers but also encourages customers to support local businesses.
